title: Fifty Thousand Names

mood.label: Heavy going 🌧️
mood.summary: A subdued, uneasy day still shadowed by Venezuela's earthquakes and Congo's Ebola, with a remarkable global aid mobilization and quiet wins in health, science and the oceans running underneath the grief.

style.genre: slow dub-ballad / spiritual-downtempo — a sampled flute tracing a low, circling topline over a sampled Rhodes-ish electric piano and a dusty, dub-spaced EmuSP12 kit with deep tape-echo, a plucked upright bass, a sine sub and a bowed-string drone underneath; A aeolian's plain natural minor sitting heavy and patient, grief held steady rather than dramatized, with one warm thread of light running through it

pattern:
stack(
  s("bd ~ ~ ~ sd ~ ~ ~, ~ ~ hh ~ ~ ~ hh ~").bank("EmuSP12").gain(0.52).room(0.32).rsize(4).delay(0.28).delaytime(0.5).delayfeedback(0.34).lpf(sine.range(1600,3600).slow(16)).swingBy(1/3,4),
  note("<[a2,c3,e3,g3] [f2,a2,c3,e3] [d3,f3,a3,c4] [e3,g3,b3,d4]>").s("piano").attack(0.02).release(0.7).gain(0.34).lpf(2100).room(0.4).rsize(5).pan(0.42),
  note("a1 ~ a1 e2 ~ f1 ~ ~ d2 ~ ~ a1 e2 ~ ~ ~").s("piano").gain(0.3).lpf(420).attack(0.01).release(0.4).hpf(40).pan(0.55),
  note("<a3 c4 e4 g4 ~ e4 d4 c4 ~ b3 a3 ~ g3 ~ e3 ~>").s("flute").gain(0.42).attack(0.06).release(0.5).lpf(sine.range(2200,4200).slow(12)).room(0.42).rsize(6).delay(0.34).delaytime(0.375).delayfeedback(0.4).off(0.25, x => x.gain(0.18).pan(0.7)).pan(0.36),
  note("<[a4,c5] ~ [g4,b4] ~ ~ [e4,g4] ~ ~>").s("sawtooth").attack(1.2).sustain(0.7).release(3).gain(0.16).lpf(sine.range(500,1500).slow(20)).room(0.6).rsize(7).hpf(120)
).slow(2)
sampleSources: Strudel default library (piano, VCSL flute, EmuSP12)

lines: Three days in and the dust won't lift,
fifty thousand names on a list nobody wants to read.
Under the slabs near Caracas a hand still moves,
and the clock the rescuers race keeps no kindness in its speed.
But look how the world leaned in —
every flag at the door with its arms held wide,
a hundred and fifty million and crews on a plane,
Brazil to Beijing on the grieving side.
and still a light we carried inside.
Ebola climbs in the Congo with no shot to give,
Vienna won't cool and the heatdome holds the line.
But plants grew medicine out past the sky,
the young don't die of the cancer now, and the oceans got six billion this time.
